title: Noah Kahan joins Fenway Music Hall of Fame after historic four-night run
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Noah Kahan performed four consecutive shows at Fenway Park, concluding with his induction into the Fenway Music Hall of Fame.
Kahan joins previous inductees Billy Joel, Paul McCartney, and the Zac Brown Band in the Fenway Hall of Fame.
2026 has been significant for Kahan, who released his fourth album and appeared on NPR’s Tiny Desk and SNL.

title: AG: Mass. to receive $730,000 from Cash App in alleged deception settlement
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Massachusetts will receive $730,000 from a $45 million multistate settlement with Cash App over alleged deceptive practices.
Block Inc., the owner of Cash App, is accused of misleading users about the app's safety and enabling fraud through promotions.
The settlement mandates improved user protection and customer service, including 24-hour support and fraud education measures.

title: Tyngsborough man may face charges after allegedly taking police boat, posing as officer
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A 63-year-old man allegedly took a Tyngsborough police boat and activated its emergency lights on a local lake.
The man is accused of impersonating a police officer, attempting to enforce boating rules on Lake Mascuppic.
Authorities plan to issue a summons for him to appear in Lowell District Court; he is not yet formally charged.

title: Photos: Stunning views of Sail Boston 250
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Over 60 international tall ships and military vessels arrived in Boston Harbor for Sail Boston 250's six-day event.
The USS Constitution led ships from over 20 countries during the Parade of Sail into Boston on Saturday.
Thousands gathered to witness the maritime event, marking the final stop of the Sail 250 tour in Boston.

title: Red Sox draft SS Jake Schaffner with No. 20 pick
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox drafted Jake Schaffner, 21, shortstop from UNC, with the No. 20 pick in the 2026 MLB Draft.
Schaffner, ranked 75th by MLB.com, had a .356 average, 6 home runs, and 50 RBI in 68 games for UNC.
He excelled defensively with a .987 fielding percentage, committing only three errors at shortstop in 2026.

title: Sail Boston 2026: The Grand Parade of Sail is rooted in rich history and tradition in the Commonwealth
author: Laurie Fullerton
bullets: The Sail Boston 2026 Grand Parade of Sail, led by the USS Constitution, featured historic ships from around the world.
The event marks the 250th anniversary with participants like the replica schooner Bluenose Two and the Spirit of Bermuda.
Spectators gathered from Castle Island to Piers Park to honor maritime history and introduce it to younger generations.

title: Brayan Bello named midseason AL ‘Cy Yuk’ winner by The Athletic for poor first-half performance
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Brayan Bello posted a 10.35 ERA in eight starts, earning the 'Cy Yuk' label from The Athletic.
Bello allowed 41 earned runs over 35.2 innings, with opponents hitting .365/.435/.623 against him.
Despite bullpen success with a 0.71 ERA, Bello was sent to Triple-A and not recalled for Saturday's game.

title: Red Sox face all too familiar question heading into MLB trade deadline: Buy or sell?
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Red Sox are on a seven-game winning streak, close to American League wildcard spot.
Boston's trade deadline decision unclear; past deadlines showed lack of firm buy/sell stance.
Craig Breslow must choose whether to strengthen team for playoffs or focus on future assets.

title: Review & Setlist: Shakira delivers high-energy nostalgia and new beginnings at TD Garden
author: Maddie Browning
bullets: Shakira performed at TD Garden, July 10, 2026, as part of her Las Mujeres Ya No Lloran World Tour.
The concert marked Shakira's return after a seven-year hiatus and featured her Grammy-winning album 'Las Mujeres Ya No Lloran'.
The setlist included hits like 'Hips Don’t Lie' and 'Waka Waka', highlighting her diverse musical styles and cultural roots.

title: Healey announces tax credit awards to build almost 700 new homes in six Mass. cities
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Healey-Driscoll administration awarded $18.6 million in tax credits to build 662 homes in six Massachusetts cities.
Projects in Fall River, Lawrence, Lowell, Pittsfield, Taunton, and Worcester will convert historic sites and underused properties.
Massachusetts' tax relief package increased HDIP funding, facilitating the creation of over 3,400 homes across 16 cities.
